name: Toe Taps
instructions: Stand behind the ball with feet shoulder-width apart
Alternate tapping the top of the ball with the sole of each foot
Keep the ball stationary, focus on rhythm
Increase speed gradually while maintaining control
tips: Keep your head up as you improve

name: Inside-Outside
instructions: Roll the ball with the sole from inside to outside
Use the inside of your foot to push it back
Repeat in a smooth rhythm
Switch feet after 30 seconds

name: La Croqueta
instructions: Push the ball with the inside of one foot
Immediately tap it with the inside of the other foot
Move laterally as you perform the skill
Keep the ball within playing distance

name: Ball Taps
instructions: Place a soccer ball stationary in front of you.
Start in an athletic stance with slight knee bends.
Lightly jump and tap the top of the ball with the sole of one foot.
Switch feet rapidly, bringing the first foot down while tapping the ball with the other foot (like running on the spot).
Keep the upper body straight and look forward rather than down at your feet.

name: Juggling
instructions: Point your toes up towards your shin to create a flat surface with your laces, which keeps the ball under control.
Kick the ball directly underneath its center to keep it from flying away.
Keep your ankle stiff upon impact to ensure consistent contact.
Maintain a slight knee bend, staying on your toes to move quickly, with your chest slightly over the ball.
Drop the ball, kick it up with one foot, and catch it. Repeat on both feet to build muscle memory.
Start by allowing one bounce between touches, then move to continuous juggling without bounces.

name: Cone Weave
instructions: Set up 6-8 cones in a line, 1 meter apart
Dribble through using inside and outside of both feet
Turn at the end and dribble back
Focus on close control, not speed
tips: Use both feet from the start

name: Scissors & Step Over
instructions: Dribble toward a cone at moderate pace
Perform a scissors move (loop foot around the ball)
Push past the cone with the outside of the opposite foot
Accelerate away after the move
tips: Drop the shoulder before the move

name: Self-Toss Control
instructions: Toss the ball at chest height
Cushion it with your chest and let it drop to your feet
Control with one touch and pass to a target
Vary the height and angle of the toss
tips: Withdraw slightly to absorb the ball

name: Thigh-Foot Combo
instructions: Toss the ball to thigh height
Cushion with your thigh
Let it drop and control with the inside of your foot
Set the ball in your chosen direction
tips: Think about your next action before the ball arrives

name: Wall Passes
instructions: Stand 3-5 meters from a wall
Pass with the inside of your foot against the wall
Control the return with your first touch
Alternate feet every 10 passes
tips: Cushion the ball on the return

name: One-Touch Passing
instructions: Stand 5 meters from a wall
Pass and receive with one touch only
Alternate between inside left and inside right
Maintain accuracy over speed
tips: Open your hips to the target

name: Laces Drive
instructions: Place the ball on the edge of the box
Approach at a slight angle
Strike through the center of the ball with your laces
Follow through toward the target
tips: Plant foot next to the ball

name: Finesse Curl
instructions: Approach at a 45-degree angle
Strike across the ball with the inside of your foot
Aim for the far post with curl
Lean slightly over the ball
tips: Wrap your foot around the ball
